binaries.title: dumpfve.sys (BitLocker Drive Encryption Crashdump Filter) - WIL feature-staging framework adds a new byte-range-clamped variant override value (not a security fix)

changed_functions.name: QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008BF0) (unpatched) → QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008C34) (patched)
key_changes: Function signature expanded from 2 parameters (uint32_t arg1, int32_t* arg2) to 4 parameters (uint32_t arg1, int32_t arg2, int32_t* arg3, int32_t* arg4) — added WIL variant query capability

Registry query table expanded from 1 entry (0x70 bytes) to support up to 2 entries (0xa8 bytes) — second entry queries a variant-override value with '_variant' suffix appended to the value name via RtlCopyUnicodeString + RtlAppendUnicodeToString

Added clamp 'if (rax_8 <= 0xff)' (cmp eax, 0xff; ja skip @ 0x1c0008da5) before storing the NEW byte-sized variant-override value into *arg4 — this value does not exist in the unpatched build, so the clamp is not a fix for a pre-existing unbounded read
Patched version initializes *arg4 = 0x80000000 (sentinel for 'not yet queried') and only overwrites it with the registry value if the value passes the <= 0xFF clamp

Both query-table entries use the same key \Registry\MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides via a single RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx call; only the value names differ (the decimal feature ID, and the same ID with a '_variant' suffix)
changed_functions.security_notes: These are the standard Windows Implementation Library (WIL) feature-staging functions (QueryFeatureOverride, EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry, EvaluateCurrentState, rbc_InitializeFeatureStaging), reading developer feature-flag overrides from the admin/SYSTEM-only FeatureManagement\Overrides key. The unpatched reader does NOT store a raw DWORD: at 0x1c0008ca6 ('mov dword [rbx], ecx') it stores only ecx which is computed as 1 (registry value == 0) or 2 (registry value != 0), i.e., a fixed {1,2} tristate. There is nothing to range-check on that path and no attacker-controlled unbounded value. The patched 'cmp eax, 0xff' at 0x1c0008da5 clamps a SECOND, newly introduced variant-override value (registry name with '_variant' suffix) that has no counterpart in the unpatched build, storing it via 'mov dword [r14], eax' at 0x1c0008dac only if <= 0xFF. This is a newer WIL revision adding a byte-sized variant value with validation built in, not remediation of a vulnerability. Reaching this code requires write access to the admin/SYSTEM-gated override key, a developer feature-flag mechanism rather than an attacker input surface.

QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1c0008c34) PATCHED key addition at 0x1c0008da1-0x1c0008daf:
0x1c0008da1: mov eax, dword [rsp+0x34]             ; eax = var_15C (second registry DWORD)
0x1c0008da5: cmp eax, 0xff                         ; BOUNDS CHECK: value <= 0xFF?
0x1c0008daa: ja 0x1c0008db5                        ; skip if > 0xFF
0x1c0008dac: mov dword [r14], eax                  ; *arg4 = value (only if <= 0xFF)
0x1c0008daf: jmp 0x1c0008db5

changed_functions.name: EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry (sub_1C0008B70) (unpatched) → EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry (sub_1C0008B3C) (patched)
key_changes: Function signature expanded from 3 parameters (int32_t arg1, int32_t arg2, int32_t* arg3) to 5 parameters (int32_t arg1, int32_t arg2, int32_t arg3, char arg4, char* arg5) — passes through the new variant byte value
Patched version initializes arg_8 = 0x80000000 (sentinel) and passes &arg_8 to the registry reader as the fourth output parameter for the bounds-checked byte value
Patched version stores the validated byte value via '*arg5 = arg_8' (mov byte [rdi], al @ 0x1c0008ba7) only when arg_8 != 0x80000000 (i.e., the registry returned a valid value <= 0xFF)
The lock cmpxchg for the primary state was moved to the caller (EvaluateFeature (sub_1C0008BBC)), decoupling the atomic state update from the registry query
Patched version uses 'mov byte [rdi], al' (byte store) for the NEW secondary output; the unpatched build has no secondary output
changed_functions.security_notes: In the unpatched version, EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry (sub_1C0008B70) calls QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008BF0) with a pointer to a stack-local int32_t (arg_8). The reader collapses the registry value to a {1,2} tristate before storing it; no raw DWORD is stored and nothing is range-checked. The lock cmpxchg at 0x1c0008bc9 atomically writes the {1,2} value to WIL's cached feature state. The patched version threads a NEW byte-sized secondary variant-override value that does not exist in the unpatched build; its clamp is validation on a new value, not a fix.

EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry (sub_1c0008b70) UNPATCHED (0x1c0008b70, 120 bytes):
0x1c0008b70: mov rax, rsp
0x1c0008b73: mov qword [rax+0x10], rbx

0x1c0008b88: mov dword [rax+0x8], edi              ; arg_8 = 0
0x1c0008b8b: mov ebx, edi
0x1c0008b8d: lea rdx, [rax+0x8]                    ; rdx = &arg_8
0x1c0008b91: setne bl                              ; rbx = (arg2 != 1)
0x1c0008b94: xor ecx, 0x74161a4e                   ; feature ID obfuscation
0x1c0008b9a: bswap ecx
0x1c0008b9c: xor ecx, 0x8fb23d4f
0x1c0008ba2: ror ecx, 0xff
0x1c0008ba5: xor ecx, 0x833ea8ff

0x1c0008bb0: test eax, eax
0x1c0008bb2: je 0x1c0008bc4

0x1c0008bba: cmp dword [rsp+0x30], 0x1

0x1c0008bc7: xor eax, eax                          ; eax = 0 (expected)
0x1c0008bc9: lock cmpxchg dword [rsi], ecx         ; atomic: if *state==0, set *state=ecx
0x1c0008bcd: mov ecx, dword [rsi]
0x1c0008bcf: cmp ecx, 0x1
0x1c0008bd2: mov rbx, qword [rsp+0x38]
0x1c0008bd7: mov rsi, qword [rsp+0x40]
0x1c0008bdc: setne dil

changed_functions.name: EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C0008B3C) (unpatched) → EvaluateFeature (sub_1C0008BBC) (patched)
key_changes: Top-level dispatcher significantly restructured: now reads additional fields from the feature table entry including arg1[3] (second state pointer at offset 0x18) and arg1[4] (byte default value at offset 0x20)
Patched version adds second state check: 'if (!result && *rdi == 0x80000000)' — requires both primary state unset AND secondary state at sentinel before querying
Patched version performs TWO atomic updates: lock cmpxchg on primary state (*rbx) AND lock cmpxchg on secondary state (*rdi) with byte value via movzx ecx, byte [rsp+0x40]
Secondary state atomic update uses 0x80000000 as the compare value (sentinel for uninitialized), preventing re-query races
The byte value for the secondary state is zero-extended via movzx before the atomic store, ensuring only a byte is written to the 32-bit state field
changed_functions.security_notes: The patched dispatcher caches a second WIL feature-state variable per feature entry, initialized to 0x80000000 (sentinel). The secondary state is only updated when the reader returns a clamped value (<= 0xFF). The movzx ecx, byte [rsp+0x40] at 0x1c0008c13 zero-extends the byte before atomic storage. In the unpatched version there is no secondary state; the primary state is a {1,2} tristate in both builds. This is the standard WIL cached-state commit extended to a new field, not a validation layer added to an attacker-reachable path.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1c0008b3c) UNPATCHED (0x1c0008b3c, 44 bytes) — simple dispatcher:
0x1c0008b3c: sub rsp, 0x28
0x1c0008b40: mov r8, qword [rcx]                   ; r8 = arg1[0] (state ptr)
0x1c0008b43: xor eax, eax
0x1c0008b45: mov r9d, dword [rcx+0x8]              ; r9 = arg1[1] (feature ID)
0x1c0008b49: mov edx, dword [rcx+0xc]              ; edx = arg1+0xc
0x1c0008b4c: mov ecx, dword [r8]                   ; ecx = *state
0x1c0008b4f: test ecx, ecx
0x1c0008b51: je 0x1c0008b5b                         ; if state==0, query
0x1c0008b5b: mov ecx, r9d
0x1c0008b5e: call 0x1c0008b70                      ; middle layer
0x1c0008b53: cmp ecx, 0x1
0x1c0008b56: setne al
0x1c0008b59: jmp 0x1c0008b63
0x1c0008b63: add rsp, 0x28
0x1c0008b67: retn

EvaluateFeature (sub_1c0008bbc) PATCHED (0x1c0008bbc, 112 bytes) — expanded with second state:

0x1c0008bee: lea rax, [rsp+0x40]

0x1c0008bf8: mov ecx, r11d
0x1c0008bfb: mov qword [rsp+0x20], rax
0x1c0008c00: call 0x1c0008b3c                      ; middle layer (now returns validated byte)
0x1c0008c05: neg eax
0x1c0008c07: sbb ecx, ecx
0x1c0008c09: neg ecx
0x1c0008c0b: inc ecx
0x1c0008c0d: xor eax, eax
0x1c0008c0f: lock cmpxchg dword [rbx], ecx         ; atomic: primary state update
0x1c0008c13: movzx ecx, byte [rsp+0x40]            ; zero-extend byte value — NEW
0x1c0008c18: mov eax, 0x80000000                   ; expected sentinel — NEW
0x1c0008c1d: lock cmpxchg dword [rdi], ecx         ; atomic: secondary state update — NEW

security_findings.entry_point: DumpPreInitialize (exported function) — called by kernel during crash dump stack initialization
call_chain: rbc_InitializeFeatureStaging (sub_1C0008CD8) (feature table iterator, 0x1c0008cd8) — iterates entries at reg_FeatureDescriptors_z
EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C0008B3C) (dispatcher, 0x1c0008b3c) — checks feature state, calls middle layer
EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry (sub_1C0008B70) (middle layer, 0x1c0008b70) — obfuscates feature ID, calls registry reader
QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008BF0) (registry reader, 0x1c0008bf0) — queries RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx; the returned DWORD is collapsed to a {1,2} tristate
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None as shipped. Original CWE-20 / CWE-697 framing does not hold: no unbounded value is stored in either build.
security_findings.description: This is the standard WIL feature-staging framework reading developer feature-flag overrides from \Registry\MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides via RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx (call at 0x1c0008c88). The returned DWORD (var_C0 at [rbp-0x69]) is NOT stored raw: it is collapsed to a fixed feature state of 1 (value == 0) or 2 (value != 0) and that {1,2} value is stored atomically via lock cmpxchg at 0x1c0008bc9. There is no unbounded value on this path and nothing for a range check to fix. The patched version (QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008C34)) adds a SECOND, newly introduced variant query (registry name with '_variant' suffix) and clamps that new byte value with 'cmp eax, 0xff; ja skip' at 0x1c0008da5. That value and its output parameter do not exist in the unpatched build, so the clamp is validation baked into a new feature, not remediation of a pre-existing vulnerability. The feature ID is obfuscated via XOR (0x74161a4e), bswap, XOR (0x8fb23d4f), ROR(0xff), XOR (0x833ea8ff) at 0x1c0008b94-0x1c0008ba5 in EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry (sub_1C0008B70); the value name is the decimal deobfuscated ID via RtlIntegerToUnicodeString at 0x1c0008c33. This obfuscation and the override key are standard WIL feature-staging mechanics.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not attacker-triggerable as a vulnerability. The FeatureManagement\Overrides key is writable only by administrator/SYSTEM by default ACL and is a developer feature-flag mechanism. Even with write access, the unpatched primary path collapses any value to a {1,2} tristate, and the patched second value is clamped to <= 0xFF; neither yields an unbounded or memory-unsafe result. The WIL staging init runs from DumpPreInitialize when the crash dump stack initializes (BSOD/hibernation).
security_findings.primitive: None. There is no memory-corruption or unbounded-value primitive. The unpatched reader emits only a {1,2} feature-enabled tristate; the patched second value is a byte clamped to <= 0xFF. Both are cached WIL feature-staging state consumed as on/off (or byte) feature flags. No attacker-controlled DWORD reaches downstream consumers.

0x1c0008bf5: mov qword [rsp+0x20], rdi

0x1c0008c0e: xor rax, rsp

0x1c0008c15: xor edi, edi                           ; edi = 0

0x1c0008c1e: mov rbx, rdx                           ; rbx = arg2 (output ptr)

0x1c0008c24: lea rax, [rbp+0x27]                    ; rax = &var_38 (string buffer)

0x1c0008c3f: xor edx, edx

0x1c0008c45: lea rcx, [rbp-0x49]                    ; rcx = &var_a8 (query table)
0x1c0008c49: call 0x1c0009200                       ; memset(table, 0, 0x70)
0x1c0008c4e: mov rax, qword [rbp-0x59]             ; rax = String.Buffer

0x1c0008c56: mov qword [rbp-0x39], rax             ; Entry[0].Name = String.Buffer

0x1c0008c6f: mov qword [rbp-0x31], rax             ; Entry[0].EntryContext = &var_C0
0x1c0008c73: xor ecx, ecx                           ; rcx = 0 (RTL_QUERY_REGISTRY_SUBKEY flag=0)
0x1c0008c75: mov dword [rbp-0x29], 0x4000000        ; Entry[0].DefaultType
0x1c0008c7c: mov qword [rbp-0x21], rdi              ; Entry[0].DefaultData = NULL
0x1c0008c80: mov dword [rbp-0x19], edi              ; Entry[0].DefaultLength = 0
0x1c0008c83: mov qword [rsp+0x20], rdi              ; arg5 = NULL (Context)
0x1c0008c88: call qword [rel 0x1c0016000]          ; RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx

0x1c0008c98: mov eax, dword [rbp-0x69]             ; eax = var_C0 (raw registry DWORD)
0x1c0008c9b: neg eax                                ; sets CF if var_C0 != 0
0x1c0008c9d: lea eax, [rdi+0x1]                     ; eax = 1

0x1c0008ca4: add ecx, eax                           ; ecx = 1 (if var_C0==0) or 2 (if !=0)

0x1c0008ca8: jmp 0x1c0008cae
; ===== FAILURE PATH =====
0x1c0008caa: mov dword [rbx], edi                   ; *arg2 = 0
0x1c0008cac: xor eax, eax                           ; return 0
; ===== EPILOGUE =====
0x1c0008cae: mov rcx, qword [rbp+0x47]
0x1c0008cb2: xor rcx, rsp
0x1c0008cb5: call 0x1c00064c0                       ; __security_check_cookie
0x1c0008cba: lea r11, [rsp+0xf0]
0x1c0008cc2: mov rbx, qword [r11+0x20]
0x1c0008cc6: mov rdi, qword [r11+0x28]
0x1c0008cca: mov rsp, r11
0x1c0008ccd: pop rbp
0x1c0008cce: retn

=== PATCHED QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1c0008c34) — BOUNDS CHECK ADDED ===
; After RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx succeeds and second value is queried:
0x1c0008da1: mov eax, dword [rsp+0x34]             ; eax = var_15C (second registry DWORD)
0x1c0008da5: cmp eax, 0xff                          ; *** BOUNDS CHECK: value must be <= 255 ***
0x1c0008daa: ja 0x1c0008db5                         ; if > 0xFF, skip storage (keep sentinel)
0x1c0008dac: mov dword [r14], eax                   ; *arg4 = value (validated byte-sized)
0x1c0008daf: jmp 0x1c0008db5

breakpoints.why: Entry to the registry reader function. RCX = obfuscated feature ID (before deobfuscation), RDX = pointer to output int32_t (arg2) where the registry result will be stored without bounds checking. Set bp here to catch all feature management override queries.

The instruction that reads the raw registry DWORD value (var_C0) from [rbp-0x69] into EAX. This is the success path AFTER RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx returns. Inspect EAX to see the registry DWORD (admin/SYSTEM-writable) before it is collapsed to the {1,2} tristate.

The store instruction 'mov dword [rbx], ecx' that writes the computed {1,2} feature state to *arg2. RBX points to the caller's stack-local variable. This is where the collapsed tristate propagates upstream.

The RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx call site. RCX=0 (RelativeTo), RDX=registry path string (FeatureManagement Overrides key), R8=query table, R9=0. Inspect the query table at R8 to see Entry[0].Name (the feature ID string) and Entry[0].EntryContext (pointer to var_C0 where the DWORD will be written).

The call from the middle layer to the registry reader. ECX at this point contains the DEOBFUSCATED feature ID (after XOR/bswap/ROR chain at 0x1c0008b94-0x1c0008ba5). RDX points to &arg_8 (stack local for registry result). RSI=arg3 (the persistent state pointer that receives the lock cmpxchg).

The lock cmpxchg that atomically stores the feature state. ECX = new value (1 or 2), EAX = 0 (expected current value). RSI = pointer to the persistent feature state field. After this instruction, the feature state is locked in.
The exported entry point called by the kernel crash dump stack. Setting a breakpoint here catches the initial invocation that triggers the entire feature management override reading chain.

watch: EAX at 0x1c0008c98 — raw registry DWORD value from FeatureManagement\Overrides (admin/SYSTEM-writable)
ECX at 0x1c0008ca6 — computed feature state (1=disabled, 2=enabled); the value is already reduced to the {1,2} tristate
RBX at 0x1c0008ca6 — pointer to output variable (*arg2) receiving the unvalidated value
[rbp-0x69] (var_C0) — the stack DWORD holding the raw registry value after RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx
RSI at 0x1c0008bc9 — pointer to persistent feature state field updated via lock cmpxchg
ECX at 0x1c0008bc9 — the value (1 or 2) being atomically stored to the feature state
RCX at 0x1c0008b94 — the obfuscated feature ID before deobfuscation (for identifying which feature is being queried)
R8 at 0x1c0008c88 — pointer to the RTL_QUERY_REGISTRY_TABLE; Entry[0].Name identifies the registry value name being queried
key_offsets: 0x1c0008c98 — mov eax, dword [rbp-0x69]: reads raw registry DWORD into EAX (admin/SYSTEM-writable value)
0x1c0008ca6 — mov dword [rbx], ecx: stores the computed {1,2} feature state (the raw DWORD is not stored)
0x1c0008bc9 — lock cmpxchg dword [rsi], ecx: atomic store to persistent feature state
0x1c0008b94-0x1c0008ba5 — feature ID deobfuscation chain (XOR 0x74161a4e, bswap, XOR 0x8fb23d4f, ROR 0xff, XOR 0x833ea8ff)
PATCHED 0x1c0008da5 — cmp eax, 0xff: the ADDED bounds check that validates registry value <= 0xFF
registry path string (second argument to RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx): \Registry\MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides
reg_FeatureDescriptors_z — start of the feature table iterated by rbc_InitializeFeatureStaging (sub_1C0008CD8)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Obtain SYSTEM or administrator privileges (required for HKLM write access). 2. Identify the feature IDs used by dumpfve.sys by examining the compiled-in table at reg_FeatureDescriptors_z — each entry has an obfuscated feature ID at offset 0x08 that is deobfuscated via XOR(bswap(XOR(id, 0x74161a4e)), 0x8fb23d4f) then ROR by 0xff then XOR 0x833ea8ff. 3. Open registry key HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides. 4. Create a REG_DWORD value whose name is the decimal string of the deobfuscated feature ID, with any value (the primary override is collapsed to a {1,2} tristate regardless of magnitude). 5. Trigger crash dump initialization — this calls DumpPreInitialize which iterates the feature table and queries each feature's registry override. This registry read runs when the feature state is 0 (not yet initialized). Note: in practice, triggering a crash dump requires inducing a BSOD or using hibernation, which invokes the crash dump stack.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At the breakpoint on 0x1c0008c98 (success path after RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx), EAX will contain the raw registry DWORD value (admin/SYSTEM-writable; e.g., 0x100 if set to 256). At 0x1c0008ca6, ECX will contain the computed state value (1 or 2), which is what is stored to *arg2; the magnitude of the registry value is not retained. At 0x1c0008bc9, the lock cmpxchg atomically commits this {1,2} state to the persistent feature state. No unbounded value reaches downstream consumers, so there is no memory-safety consequence on this path. The patched cmp eax, 0xff at 0x1c0008da5 applies only to the separate variant-override value, which does not exist in the unpatched build.
debugging_aids.notes: The feature IDs are obfuscated in the binary table at reg_FeatureDescriptors_z. To identify them, breakpoint at 0x1c0008b94 (start of deobfuscation) where ECX contains the raw obfuscated ID, then step through the XOR/bswap/ROR chain to get the plaintext feature ID. The registry value name is the decimal representation of this plaintext ID. Setting these registry values requires admin/SYSTEM privileges; this is a developer feature-flag mechanism, not an unprivileged input surface. The driver is dumpfve.sys (BitLocker Drive Encryption Crashdump Filter), loaded during crash dump stack initialization. The __security_cookie stack guard is present in the function (at 0x1c0008c07-0x1c0008c11).

summary: The three changed functions are the standard Windows Implementation Library (WIL) feature-staging framework in dumpfve.sys (BitLocker Drive Encryption Crashdump Filter), reading developer feature-flag overrides from \Registry\MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\Microsoft\FeatureManagement\Overrides via RtlQueryRegistryValuesEx. The original report framed this as a missing-bounds-check vulnerability; that framing does not hold. The unpatched registry reader (QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008BF0) at 0x1c0008bf0) does not store a raw DWORD — at 0x1c0008ca6 ('mov dword [rbx], ecx') it stores only a fixed {1,2} tristate (1 if the value is 0, else 2), so there is no unbounded value and nothing to range-check. The patched version (QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008C34)) adds a SECOND, newly introduced variant-override value (registry name with '_variant' suffix) and clamps it with 'cmp eax, 0xff; ja skip' at 0x1c0008da5; that value and its output parameter do not exist in the unpatched build, so the clamp is validation baked into a new feature rather than remediation of a pre-existing flaw. The chain DumpPreInitialize → rbc_InitializeFeatureStaging (sub_1C0008CD8) → EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C0008B3C) → EvaluateCurrentStateFromRegistry (sub_1C0008B70) → QueryFeatureOverride (sub_1C0008BF0) is the WIL cached-feature-state init. The override key is admin/SYSTEM-only by design. Net assessment: framework evolution, not a security fix; no attacker-controlled unbounded value in either build.
